segments.text: Only ancient India could design this. It looks like a simple pot, but the design is really clever. Sometimes washing hands is terrifying. If your hands are
dirty, you make the mug dirty and it's a loop. But this rim is shaped to lock onto your wrist. You lift, you pour, and wash without your fingers ever touching the pot. Zero contact, 100% hygiene.
Next is stability. Put a bottle on a train and it falls. But this uses a calculated center of gravity. Shake it, push it, put it on a moving bulock cart,
it refuses to fall. It's basically a gyroscope made of brass. And the last one fixes space because they did not carry cups. The lid is not just a cover.
It's a perfectly shaped drinking glass [music] hidden in plain sight. One pot, three solutions. For more design wisdom, subscribe.
